reasoning: The informal statement claims that the additive group of ℚ is not cyclic. The Lean statement `¬ (IsAddCyclic ℚ)` uses Mathlib's `IsAddCyclic`, which is defined (in Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Defs.lean) as: `∃ g : G, Function.Surjective (· • g : ℤ → G)` — i.e., the group has a generator, matching the standard definition of a cyclic (additive) group. The natural additive group structure on ℚ is the canonical one implied by the informal statement. Thus the Lean theorem asserts exactly the same mathematical content as the informal claim, neither stronger nor weaker.
